mappings.reason: Deploying a Data Loss Prevention Solution directly implements DM-22's requirement for DLP capabilities to protect sensitive information across stored, transmitted, and processed states.
Encrypting sensitive data in transit protects information during transmission, satisfying the 'transmitted' protection requirement of DM-22.
Encrypting sensitive data at rest protects information on servers and databases, satisfying the 'stored' protection requirement of DM-22.
Encrypting data on end-user devices protects sensitive information on portable assets, satisfying the 'stored' protection requirement of DM-22.
Encrypting data on removable media protects sensitive information on portable storage, satisfying the 'stored' protection requirement of DM-22.
Configuring data access control lists prevents unauthorized access to sensitive data, satisfying the 'protect' objective of DM-22.
Securely disposing of data prevents data loss at the end of the data lifecycle, satisfying the 'processed' protection requirement of DM-22.
Remote wipe capability protects sensitive data on lost or stolen devices by destroying it, satisfying the 'stored' protection requirement of DM-22.
Network-based URL filters can block data exfiltration channels, satisfying the 'transmitted' protection requirement of DM-22.
Blocking unnecessary file types at the email gateway prevents data exfiltration via email, satisfying the 'transmitted' protection requirement of DM-22.
Application layer filtering can enforce data exfiltration policies at the gateway, satisfying the 'transmitted' protection requirement of DM-22.
